description: Etapa 2: Cash events no event ledger. Cada linha do extrato vira um evento cash_* imutavel. Permite reconciliacao automatica: sum(cash) == extrato.

userStories.title: Migration 008: reference_id column + indexes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the reference_id column and cash event indexes added to payment_events table.
acceptanceCriteria: File migrations/008_unified_ledger.sql created
SQL adds: ALTER TABLE payment_events ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS reference_id TEXT
SQL backfills: UPDATE payment_events SET reference_id = ml_payment_id::text WHERE reference_id IS NULL
SQL sets: ALTER TABLE payment_events ALTER COLUMN ml_payment_id SET DEFAULT 0
SQL creates index: idx_pe_seller_ref ON payment_events (seller_slug, reference_id) WHERE reference_id IS NOT NULL
SQL creates index: idx_pe_cash_events ON payment_events (seller_slug, event_date) WHERE event_type LIKE 'cash_%'
Migration applied to Supabase via mcp__supabase__apply_migration
Verification: SELECT reference_id FROM payment_events LIMIT 5 returns non-null values
python3 -m pytest passes (152+ tests)
userStories.notes: Read migrations/006_payment_events.sql for existing schema. Read rfcs/plano-execucao/03-fase1-cash-events.md Passo 1 for exact SQL.

userStories.title: Add 6 cash event types + protect existing queries
userStories.description: As a system, I need 6 new cash_* event types in the ledger, support for 'any' sign validation, reference_id parameter in record_event(), and existing DRE/status queries protected from contamination.
acceptanceCriteria: 6 event types added to EVENT_TYPES dict in app/services/event_ledger.py: cash_release (positive), cash_expense (negative), cash_income (positive), cash_transfer_out (negative), cash_transfer_in (positive), cash_internal (any)
Comment added above cash types: '# Cash events — competencia_date = event_date (caixa, NAO competencia real). DRE queries MUST exclude these.'
validate_event() updated: if expected == 'any': pass (accepts any sign)
record_event() gains new optional parameter: reference_id: str | None = None
record_event() includes reference_id in the row dict: reference_id = reference_id if reference_id is not None else str(ml_payment_id)
get_dre_summary(): after fetching rows, filter out rows where event_type starts with 'cash_' or 'expense_'
get_payment_statuses(): after fetching rows, filter out rows where event_type starts with 'cash_' or 'expense_'
python3 -m pytest passes (152+ tests, DRE values unchanged)
userStories.notes: Read app/services/event_ledger.py fully. The EVENT_TYPES dict is near the top. validate_event() checks sign constraints. get_dre_summary() is around line 350 — it queries by competencia_date without filtering event_type. get_payment_statuses() is around line 313. CRITICAL: cash events use competencia_date = event_date, so without the filter they would contaminate DRE results.

userStories.title: record_cash_event() and get_cash_summary() functions
userStories.description: As a system, I need functions to record cash events with correct idempotency key (including abbreviation) and query cash summaries by date range.
acceptanceCriteria: Function record_cash_event(seller_slug, reference_id, event_type, signed_amount, event_date, extrato_type, expense_type_abbrev='xx', metadata=None) added to event_ledger.py
Validates event_type starts with 'cash_' (raises ValueError otherwise)
Idempotency key format: {seller}:{ref_id}:{event_type}:{date}:{abbrev} (5 parts)
ml_payment_id set via int(reference_id) with fallback to 0
competencia_date set equal to event_date (documented in docstring)
source set to 'extrato'
Metadata includes extrato_type and source='account_statement'
Function get_cash_summary(seller_slug, date_from, date_to) added
get_cash_summary uses .range() pagination (page_limit=1000)
get_cash_summary returns dict like {cash_release: 12345.67, cash_expense: -1234.56}
get_cash_summary filters only event_type starting with 'cash_'
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: Read rfcs/plano-execucao/03-fase1-cash-events.md Passo 4 and Passo 7 for exact function signatures. The abbreviation in the idempotency key prevents collision when two different transaction types generate the same cash event type for the same ref_id on the same day.

userStories.title: Cash events test suite
userStories.description: As a developer, I need comprehensive tests for cash event types, idempotency, and query protection.
acceptanceCriteria: File testes/test_cash_events.py created with 9 tests
test_cash_event_types_exist: all 6 cash types in EVENT_TYPES
test_validate_cash_any_sign: cash_internal accepts both positive and negative
test_record_cash_event_idempotency_includes_date: same ref_id different dates produce different idem keys
test_record_cash_event_idempotency_includes_abbrev: same ref_id same date different abbrev produce different idem keys
test_no_collision_different_types_same_ref: two expense types with same ref_id/day do not collide
test_get_dre_summary_excludes_cash: get_dre_summary does not return cash events
test_get_payment_statuses_excludes_cash: get_payment_statuses does not return cash events
test_get_cash_summary: correct aggregation by type
test_skip_mapping_not_all_internal: verifies PIX enviado is NOT cash_internal
python3 -m pytest passes (152 existing + 9 new = 161+ tests)
userStories.notes: Read testes/test_event_ledger.py for existing test patterns. Tests use conftest.py fixtures and mock Supabase. Follow the same pattern of mocking db calls. The SKIP_TO_CASH_TYPE and CASH_TYPE_MAP dicts should be importable from wherever the ingestion script defines them (or define in event_ledger.py).

userStories.title: Extrato ingestion script for cash events
userStories.description: As an operator, I need a script that reads the extrato CSV and creates cash events in the ledger with correct type mappings and validates the reconciliation invariant.
acceptanceCriteria: Script testes/ingest_extrato_to_ledger.py created
Accepts CLI args: --seller 141air --month jan2026 [--dry-run]
CASH_TYPE_MAP has 17 entries (NO deposito_avulso or cashback — these are dead mappings)
SKIP_TO_CASH_TYPE has 13 entries with per-rule mapping: PIX/transferencia/pagamento -> cash_transfer_out, compra -> cash_expense, transferencia de saldo/reservado/renda -> cash_internal
SKIP_TO_CASH_TYPE includes accented keys: transferência enviada, transferência de saldo
SKIP_ABBREV has 13 entries matching SKIP_TO_CASH_TYPE keys
For _CHECK_PAYMENTS: if ref_id in payment_events -> cash_release with abbrev 'cr'
Dry-run mode: counts and validates without writing, prints total lines and count per cash event type and SUM
Real run for 141air jan2026: creates 690 cash events (verify via SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM payment_events WHERE seller_slug = '141air' AND event_type LIKE 'cash_%')
Sum verification: SELECT ROUND(SUM(signed_amount)::numeric, 2) FROM payment_events WHERE seller_slug = '141air' AND event_type LIKE 'cash_%' returns -3385.83
Re-run is idempotent: reports '0 newly ingested, 690 already exist'

DRE unchanged: SELECT event_type, ROUND(SUM(signed_amount)::numeric, 2) FROM payment_events WHERE seller_slug = '141air' AND event_type NOT LIKE 'cash_%' AND event_type NOT LIKE 'expense_%' AND competencia_date BETWEEN '2026-01-01' AND '2026-01-31' GROUP BY event_type returns same values as before ingestion
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: Read rfcs/plano-execucao/03-fase1-cash-events.md Passo 5 and Passo 6 for complete mappings and script flow. Read app/services/extrato_ingester.py lines 88-149 for EXTRATO_CLASSIFICATION_RULES, lines 151-184 for _CHECK_PAYMENTS_FALLBACK and _EXPENSE_TYPE_ABBREV, lines 243-327 for _parse_account_statement(). Extrato CSV is at testes/data/extratos/extrato janeiro 141Air.csv. The script must import and reuse these existing functions, NOT reimplement them. Reference data: initial_balance=4476.23, final_balance=1090.40, net=-3385.83, 690 lines.
